title: B.C. Strengthens Biofuel Industry With Canadian-Content Rules
content: B.C. is amending its Low Carbon Fuels Act to require more Canadian-produced biofuels in transportation fuels, supporting local producers, jobs, and energy security. Effective Jan. 1, 2026, gasoline must contain 5% Canadian biofuels, and dieselâ€™s renewable content will rise to 8% by April 1, 2025. This move counters U.S. subsidies, stabilizes the market, and benefits companies like Tidewater Renewables, Parkland, and Consolidated Biofuels. Industry leaders welcome the policy, citing its role in leveling the playing field, reducing reliance on imports, and reinforcing B.C.â€™s leadership in clean energy and emissions reduction.
author: Analysts Account
